have h₁ : (fun x => Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf x)) (kerLift f x) = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x)) := rfl
      have h₂ : (fun x => Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf x)) (kerLift f x) = x := by
        -- Use the left inverse property
        have h₃ : Function.LeftInverse (fun x => Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf x)) (kerLift f) := by
          intro x
          have h₄ : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x))) = kerLift f x := by
            rw [rightInverse_surjInv hf]
            <;> rfl
          -- Prove that (fun x => Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf x)) is a left inverse of kerLift f
          have h₅ : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x))) = kerLift f x := by
            rw [rightInverse_surjInv hf]
            <;> rfl
          -- Use the injectivity of kerLift f to conclude the proof

            apply kerLift_injective f
            simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
            <;> simp_all [kerLift_mk]
            <;> simp_all [hf]
            <;> aesop
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        -- Apply the left inverse property to x
        have h₇ : (fun x => Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf x)) (kerLift f x) = x := h₃ x
        exact h₇
      simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
      <;> aesop
  }

have h₅ : (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x)) : R ⧸ ker f) = x := by
          have h₆ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) ≫ (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f)) = f := by

          have h₇ : f (surjInv hf (kerLift f x)) = kerLift f x := by

              _ = (f : R →+* S) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x)) := by
                have h₉ : (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f)) = f⁻¹ (RingHom.comp (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f)) f) := by
                  simp_all [RingHom.ext_iff]
                  <;> aesop
                simp_all [RingHom.ext_iff]
                <;> aesop
              _ = kerLift f x := by simp [h₇]
              _ = (RingHom.map (kerLift f) : R ⧸ ker f → S) x := by simp_all [RingHom.ext_iff]
              _ = x := by
                have h₁₀ : (RingHom.map (kerLift f) : R ⧸ ker f → S) = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) := by
                  simp_all [RingHom.ext_iff]
                  <;> aesop
                simp_all [RingHom.ext_iff]
                <;> aesop
          exact h₈
        simp_all [Function.LeftInverse]
        <;> aesop
      exact h₃ x
  }
